spans.text: from the cliff the falcon wheels her way
the skies , And far away the glistening vapours sail , Down the rough steep the accustomed hedger hies , And the stream winds in brightness through the vale
their foreheads hoar , That meet the earliest sunbeams of the sky
the earliest sunbeams of the sky
the fragrance of the tepid morn Round their torn breasts and throbbing temples
To breathe of comfort never to return
dreams arise , Awhile forgetful of their pain they gaze , A transient lustre lights their faded eyes , And over their cheek the tender hectic plays
soft the wakeful nerve's according string
yonder spring may hang in mute distress
a friend , And bearing still the arrow at his heart
The gay companion of our stripling prime
heard the merry bells by Isis ' stream
our way was strewed with fairy flowers
how days and hours have passed along
all drooping pale , Raised from the couch of sickness and of woe
he whom late I saw all drooping pale , Raised from the couch of sickness and of woe
of woodland grey , Can wake the wonted sense of pure delight
their silent path pursue Back to the crowded world where fortune guides : Clifton , to thy white rocks and woods adieu
to the crowded world where fortune guides
